title: Why New Agents Evaluate eXp Realty Carefully
excerpt: Find out why new real estate agents choose eXp Realty – real training, certified mentorship, zero fees, sponsor team support & passive income.
content:
Key Takeaway: New real estate agents join eXp Realty for its brokerage structure, which combines certified mentorship, centralized training delivery, technology access, standardized fees, and optional sponsor-provided systems. These elements differ from traditional franchise brokerages and require active participation by the agent.

TL;DR About Why New Agents Join eXp Realty

  • New agents need practical training beyond licensing classes
  • Certified mentors guide agents through first transactions
  • FastCap and live training programs focus on early transaction skills
  • No franchise, royalty, or desk fees drain early income
  • Full CRM, website, and tools are included
  • Sponsor teams may provide additional systems, training, or accountability

After licensing, new real estate agents must choose a brokerage that determines cost structure, training access, mentorship availability, technology resources, and ongoing support. These early decisions affect how quickly agents become operational and how much overhead they carry while learning the business.

This article explains how why new agents join eXp Realty fits into the broader eXp Realty Fit ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your index:

Core Support Elements New Agents Typically Evaluate

Let’s kill a few myths real quick: you don’t need a fancy lobby, a shiny franchise name, or an “atta boy” from your broker as they hand you a bill for desk fees. You need real stuff that actually helps you make money:

  • Real training: The kind that picks up where your license class left off (because let’s face it: your pre-license class basically taught you how not to get sued… and that’s it).
  • Hands-on mentorship: An actual experienced agent who helps you get clients and close deals – not one who vanishes when your first contract blows up.
  • Business systems: CRMs, websites, lead generation tools, and marketing systems already set up, so you’re not duct-taping it together while trying to find your next buyer.
  • Support: Brokers, transaction managers, and admin staff who answer you before your deal implodes – not after.
  • Modern marketing: Social media, video, online lead generation – not cold-calling scripts from 1987.

Sadly, most brokerages hand you a desk, a bill, and a “good luck out there.” Which is probably why so many new agents don’t make it past their first year.

How eXp Realty Structures Training and Support for New Agents

Here’s where eXp Realty stops playing around. They didn’t build this brokerage for agents who “want to figure it out someday.” They built it for new real estate agents who need an actual roadmap – not another motivational quote and a welcome packet.

Certified Mentor Program

Every new eXp Realty agent gets assigned an eXp certified mentor who’s walked this road before – and survived. This isn’t some random office manager who’s too busy managing office drama. This mentorship structure provides transaction guidance, contract review, and process clarification during an agent’s initial transactions, with scope and involvement defined by program requirements.

Training Systems

The training here isn’t just “how to fill out forms.” eXp Realty’s new real estate agent training is stacked:

  • Go Curriculum On-Demand: Business-building training that covers all the stuff your licensing class conveniently skipped.
  • FastCap Program: 6 weeks of live coaching that helps new agents signed contracts because watching your bank account slowly die isn’t a strategy.
  • 60+ Hours of Live Weekly Training: Real-world topics like contracts, lead generation, marketing, negotiation, business planning, and more. All taught by top eXp agents and staff.
  • Bonus Sponsor Team Training: If you choose a powerful eXp Realty sponsor, many offer even more weekly live coaching stacked on top of eXp’s core training.

On-Demand Training Library

Access hundreds of hours of on-demand training 24/7 – because your business questions don’t only show up Monday through Friday, 9 to 5. Sponsor team libraries often add even more content so you’ve got the answers you need when you need them.

Broker and Transaction Support

Full-time state brokers and transaction managers are standing by every single business day via eXp World, Workplace Chat, or email. When your deal goes sideways (because at some point it will), you’re not left sending smoke signals and praying for a callback.

Collaboration & Community

Unlike traditional real estate brokerages that limit you to whoever happens to sit in your office, eXp Realty connects you to a national (and international) cloud-based community of eXp Realty agents. You learn directly from some of the most productive agents in the company – not just whoever drew the short straw at Tuesday’s office meeting.

How eXp Realty’s Brokerage Model Differs From Traditional Offices

Most real estate brokerages still operate like it’s 1998 – complete with overpriced office space, mystery fees, and “team leaders” who take half your check while offering “mentorship” that mostly consists of vague advice like work harder.

Here’s the reality check:

Traditional Brokerages:

  • Desk fees. Franchise fees. Royalty fees. Admin fees. (Basically, a full-time job just paying your brokerage.)
  • Team leaders who split your commission but aren’t actually in the trenches helping you build your business.
  • Oh, and by the way, CRMs, websites, and marketing tools? Those often cost extra. Because apparently, closing deals wasn’t expensive enough already.

eXp Realty:

  • You start at an 80/20 commission split with a $16,000 cap  –  even as a new real estate agent.
  • You get a path to earn your $16,000 cap back in company stock – yep, earning 100% for the year.
  • No franchise fees. No royalty fees. No desk fees. No surprise monthly invoices that feel like subscription hell.
  • Your full CRM (choose BoldTrail, Lofty, or Cloze), your website (when you choose BoldTrail or Lofty), and full business systems are included in your $85/month fee.
  • You’re allowed (and encouraged) to build your personal brand from day one – because the brokerage shouldn’t own your face.
  • You can partner with a powerful sponsor team (aka your eXp sponsor) who loads you up with extra free resources, coaching, and lead-generation tools.
  • Want extra accountability? You can also join a production real estate team within eXp for even more structure – only if you want it.

At eXp Realty, you get the actual tools, support, and systems you need to grow your business – without getting crushed under fees before your first deal even closes.

Common Observations Reported By New eXp Realty Agents

New agents evaluating eXp Realty often report similar early impressions based on their onboarding and first transactions. These observations are drawn from publicly available agent feedback and recurring themes rather than individual guarantees or outcomes.

New agents commonly note that training is accessible in multiple formats, including live sessions and on-demand classes, allowing them to engage without relying on physical office attendance. Some agents describe the ability to work remotely while still accessing structured education as a meaningful shift from traditional office-based learning.

Mentorship is frequently referenced as a source of early guidance, particularly around contracts, compliance, and transaction flow. Agents often mention that having defined mentor and broker access helps clarify questions that arise during initial deals.

Support availability is another recurring theme. New agents regularly point to consistent access to brokers and staff through cloud-based systems as reducing uncertainty when navigating unfamiliar processes. This access is often contrasted with experiences at brokerages where support depends on office presence or limited availability.

Finally, agents commonly describe increased clarity around expectations. Rather than relying on informal office culture, new agents often report that training paths, tools, and support channels are clearly defined, which can influence confidence during the transition from licensing to active production.

What Agents Also Ask About Being a New Agent at eXp Realty

Why do new agents feel more confident starting at eXp Realty?

New agents often feel more confident at eXp Realty because expectations, systems, and support paths are clearly defined. Instead of relying on informal office culture, agents know where to get training, broker support, and mentorship, which reduces uncertainty during the most fragile early months.

What makes eXp Realty different from other brokerages for first-year agents?

eXp Realty differs by replacing office dependency with structured systems, live training, and certified mentors. New agents are not dependent on a single office broker or manager, which creates more consistent access to help regardless of location, schedule, or market size.

Who tends to succeed fastest as a new agent at eXp Realty?

New agents who engage consistently with available training, participate in mentorship programs, ask operational questions early, and follow established systems tend to progress more quickly at eXp Realty. The brokerage model is structured around proactive participation, making it better aligned with agents who take responsibility for learning workflows, tools, and support resources.

What concerns do new agents usually have before joining eXp Realty?

Common concerns include lack of a physical office, fear of being unsupported, and uncertainty about learning contracts remotely. New agents often question whether virtual support can replace in-person access, making it important to understand how mentorship and broker availability actually function at eXp.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty for new agents is designed to address early-career challenges such as training gaps, deal guidance, startup costs, and access to support, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience. 

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

Frequently Asked Questions

Why do so many new agents join eXp Realty?

New agents join eXp Realty for access to structured training programs, certified mentorship, centralized technology, and broker support delivered through a cloud-based platform. Lower startup costs and the absence of franchise or desk fees reduce early financial pressure, while sponsor teams can add additional coaching and accountability.

What kind of training does eXp Realty offer new agents?

eXp Realty offers live weekly classes, on-demand education, FastCap coaching programs, and mentor-guided transaction support. Training focuses on contracts, lead generation, marketing, negotiations, and compliance, providing practical instruction beyond pre-licensing coursework.

How does eXp Realty’s mentor program work for new agents?

Every new agent is assigned a local Certified eXp Mentor who provides guidance through the first transactions. Mentors review contracts, explain processes, and help agents avoid costly mistakes, offering real-world support during the most vulnerable phase of an agent’s career.

Does eXp Realty charge franchise or desk fees?

No. eXp Realty does not charge franchise fees, royalty fees, or desk fees. New agents pay a monthly technology fee and standard transaction fees, which are predictable and capped, helping agents avoid accumulating high overhead before closing consistent deals.

How does the Smart Agent Alliance sponsor team help new agents?

Smart Agent Alliance provides additional live coaching, weekly Zoom sessions, systems training, and community support beyond core eXp resources. New agents gain access to proven strategies, collaboration, and tools designed to shorten the learning curve and reduce isolation.

excerpt: Learn how eXp Realty helps new agents generate leads, get clients, and close deals faster with proven systems, mentorship, and marketing tools.
content:
Key Takeaways: Most new real estate agents fail due to inconsistent lead generation, not lack of effort. eXp Realty provides new agents with built-in lead systems, CRMs, automation, training, and optional corporate lead sources designed to help them generate clients and close deals faster. 

TL;DR About eXp Realty New Agent Leads

  • New agents struggle most with predictable lead flow, not licensing or contracts
  • eXp Realty provides CRMs, IDX websites, automation, and lead capture tools from day one
  • BoldTrail or Lofty CRM includes automated follow-up and nurture campaigns
  • Daily live training and a large on-demand library support lead conversion skills
  • eXp’s Revenos division offers optional corporate and referral-based lead opportunities
  • Sponsor teams influence how well tools and training are implemented in practice
  • New agents who use complete systems tend to gain confidence and momentum faster

If you are a new real estate agent, the real question is not whether you are motivated. It is whether you know where your next client is coming from.

Most licensing programs teach compliance, not client acquisition. New agents leave class knowing how to avoid lawsuits, but not how to build a pipeline. Traditional brokerages often rely on name recognition while offering little structure for consistent lead generation.

eXp Realty approaches this differently. Instead of asking new agents to “figure it out,” the platform is built around systems, automation, and training designed to help agents generate leads, follow up consistently, and convert opportunities into closings.

This article explains how eXp Realty new agent leads fit into the broader eXp Realty Leads ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

The Truth About Lead Generation as a New Agent

Let’s be real  –  most real estate brokerages aren’t handing you leads. Full stop.

At a lot of traditional brokerages, the “lead generation plan” for new real estate agents usually sounds like:

  • “Call your sphere  –  your broke cousin might be buying!”
  • “Knock on doors  –  because nothing says 2025 like bothering strangers at dinner.”
  • “Cold call expired listings  –  hope you like voicemails.”
  • “Sit in the office  –  and pray someone walks in who isn’t lost.”

Sure, these old-school tactics worked back when online real estate brokerages didn’t exist and the MLS was still printed on paper. But for most new agents today, these strategies are exhausting, unpredictable, and usually lead to burnout long before your first closing.

New Real Estate Agents Need Actual Systems  –  not just more hustle.

What you really need is:

  • Predictable lead sources that fill your pipeline consistently.
  • Automation that follows up with leads while you’re actually working with clients.
  • Marketing systems that attract buyers and sellers where they live  –  online, on social, and inside your CRM.

This is exactly why eXp Realty agents  –  especially brand new ones  –  have such a major advantage out of the gate.

The Lead Generation Tools eXp Realty Provides

At eXp Realty, you’re not left duct-taping your business together from random YouTube tutorials. From day one, new real estate agents get real tools designed to help you generate leads, automate follow-up, and close deals  –  without reinventing the wheel.

BoldTrail or Lofty CRM  –  Included

  • Full lead management system built for real estate agents.
  • Automated follow-up via text, email, and phone  –  while you’re out showing homes.
  • Smart nurture campaigns to warm up cold leads automatically.
  • Both CRMs integrate seamlessly with your marketing, so you’re not manually chasing every lead.

IDX Website  –  Included when you choose BoldTrail or Lofty

  • A full property search website branded to you.
  • Built-in lead capture that helps convert casual visitors into clients.
  • Create custom landing pages for specific listings, neighborhoods, or niches  –  so you can target the right buyers and sellers.

Lead Capture & Automation Tools

  • Text-code lead capture for signs, ads, and open houses.
  • Open house lead registration systems that actually work.
  • Social media lead gen integrations to pull prospects straight from your ads.
  • Facebook ad platform integrations (optional paid programs) for additional buyer and seller leads.

Daily Live Training on Lead Generation
You’re not just handed tools  –  you’re shown exactly how to use them.

  • Open house strategies that actually generate buyers.
  • How to run powerful buyer consultations and convert leads.
  • Listing presentations that win you the listing every time.
  • Social media marketing that brings clients to you.
  • Paid lead generation training for Facebook, Google, and Zillow platforms.
  • Sphere of influence conversion strategies to turn your network into business.

On-Demand Training Library

  • 60+ live classes every single week.
  • 400+ on-demand courses inside eXp University to level up whenever you need.

The Revenos Advantage: Corporate Leads You Won’t Find Anywhere Else

Here’s something no other brokerage offers: a full division at eXp Realty focused entirely on generating high-quality leads for its agents. The Revenos Division provides eXp agents access to exclusive corporate, relocation, military, iBuyer, and institutional investor leads — not low-quality, recycled internet leads you’re forced to fight for at other brokerages.

In 2024 alone, Revenos leads helped generate over $2 billion in closed sales for eXp agents. Through eight specialized programs like ExpressOffers, eXp Relocation, Honor Military Rewards, Affinity Corporate Programs, Institutional Investment Services, REO, Bundle Select, and Making It Rain, agents can choose the lead types that fit their business goals. Certification programs are free for eXp agents and allow you to qualify for lead opportunities that many agents at other brokerages don’t even have access to.

Unlike expensive third-party leads, Revenos charges only a referral fee at closing (usually between 25-55%), giving you the chance to scale quickly with high-converting lead sources and no upfront marketing expense.

Bottom line: Revenos gives eXp agents a level of built-in lead opportunity no other brokerage can match.

The Extra Lead Generation Advantage: Your Sponsor Team

Here’s what most new eXp Realty agents don’t realize upfront: not every eXp sponsor offers the same support, and most offer no support at all. While every agent gets eXp’s corporate tools, the best eXp sponsors go way beyond that.

When you partner with teams like Smart Agent Alliance (also part of the Wolf Pack), you get a lot more lead generation firepower. More on that below.

Bottom line: With the right eXp sponsor, you’re not just getting leads  –  you’re building a lead generation machine.

Why New Agents at eXp Gain Confidence Faster

Let’s be honest  –  confidence in real estate doesn’t come from reading more contracts. It comes from knowing you’ve got a system that actually works.

When you have predictable lead generation built into your business from day one:

  • You’re not waking up every morning wondering where your next buyer or seller is hiding.
  • You follow a repeatable pipeline-building plan that fills your CRM while you focus on closings.
  • You know exactly what to say, when to say it, and how to convert because you’ve rehearsed it in live coaching and weekly trainings.
  • Instead of spinning your wheels for months, you’re stacking contracts within your first 90 days.

This is why eXp Realty agents, especially those who plug into strong sponsor teams like Smart Agent Alliance, are closing deals while most new agents are still watching YouTube videos trying to figure it out.

Lead Generation Is the #1 Reason New Agents Succeed or Quit

Let’s lay it out: most new real estate agents don’t quit because they’re lazy. They quit because they’ve got zero clue where their next client is coming from. Without a system, it’s chaos.

The stats don’t lie:

  • Only 2.4% of leads convert to deals  –  meaning you need a steady pipeline to land even a few closings .
  • New agents burn through leads, not money  –  but 25% spend under $50/month on lead generation, and 46% keep it under $250

The reality check:

  • 71% of buyers only contact one agent  –  almost like speed-dating in real estate
  • Referrals have 25% higher lifetime value and cost far less than finding new clients

Modern marketing dominates:

  • 89% of Realtors use Facebook for leads, and 54% say social media is their top tech tool
  • Email marketing returns $42 for every $1 spent

The takeaway? Without predictable leads, no amount of grind, hustle, or “being available” will save you.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty New Agent Leads

Does eXp Realty actually help new agents get clients faster?

eXp Realty does not guarantee transactions, but it provides systems that help new agents build momentum sooner. CRMs, IDX websites, automation, and daily training support consistent lead generation and follow-up, which can reduce the time between licensing and first closings when used consistently.

How does eXp Realty compare to traditional brokerages for new agents?

Traditional brokerages often emphasize independence early, expecting new agents to build systems on their own. eXp Realty focuses on platform access, automation, and shared infrastructure, giving new agents structured tools and training instead of relying primarily on office traffic or personal networks.

Is eXp Realty designed for brand-new agents or experienced agents only?

eXp Realty is structured to support agents at different career stages. New agents typically use the platform’s training, CRM, and automation to build foundational skills, while experienced agents use the same systems to scale. The tools are shared, but how they are used varies by experience level.

Do sponsor teams matter for new agent success at eXp?

Yes. While the brokerage platform is the same, sponsor teams vary in onboarding support, accountability, and system guidance. This difference often affects how quickly new agents implement tools and gain confidence.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty’s New Agent Leads are designed to help newly licensed agents access early lead opportunities, but they do not operate in isolation or replace the broader eXp Realty experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do new agents at eXp Realty receive a CRM?

Yes. New eXp Realty agents have access to brokerage-provided CRM options such as BoldTrail or Lofty. These platforms include lead management, automated follow-up, basic marketing workflows, and nurture campaigns. Agents may choose which CRM best fits their business, but access to CRM tools is part of the core brokerage offering.

Is an IDX website included for new eXp agents?

Yes. Agents using BoldTrail or Lofty receive a branded IDX website as part of the platform. These sites include lead capture functionality and integrate directly with the agent’s CRM. The IDX site supports organic lead capture rather than serving as a replacement for active prospecting or marketing.

Are Revenos leads required for new agents?

No. Revenos is eXp Realty’s optional corporate lead division and is not required for new agents. Participation depends on program eligibility and certification. New agents may build their business using referrals, organic lead strategies, or other tools without enrolling in any Revenos program.

Do new agents have to purchase leads to succeed at eXp Realty?

No. Many eXp agents build successful businesses using organic marketing, referrals, sphere of influence, and automated follow-up systems. Paid lead programs exist as optional accelerators, not requirements. Agents are not required to purchase leads in order to remain active or productive at eXp.

Does sponsor support change what tools new agents receive?

No. All eXp Realty agents receive the same brokerage tools and platform access regardless of sponsor. Sponsor support affects onboarding guidance, training, implementation help, and community access, but it does not change which CRM, websites, or core systems an agent is entitled to use.

Structural Limitations New Agents Encounter At Traditional Brokerages

Most traditional real estate brokerages are still running playbooks from 1998. And if you’re a new agent trying to actually build a business in 2025, that’s a problem.

High Desk & Franchise Fees:
Many traditional brokerages have high fees charged whether agents are producing income or not.

“Figure It Out” Mentorship:
Congrats  –  you’ve been assigned a real estate mentor. Someone who might text you back, eventually, if their own deals don’t get in the way.

The big plan? Cold calls, door knocking, and open houses. You know, the stuff your prospects block on their phones and avoid like free mall kiosks. Meanwhile, real training on online lead generation, real estate social media marketing, and personal branding are often not covered.

High Splits & Royalty Fees:
Starting out with 50/50 or 60/40 splits, plus 6-8% franchise fees, means you’re handing over huge chunks of every commission, while doing 100% of the work.

And don’t forget the in-person office drama with potentially passive-aggressive top producers, desk wars, and enough distractions to kill your productivity before you even log into the MLS.

eXp Realty wasn’t built to recycle the same broken brokerage model with a shinier logo. It was built to help new agents succeed. Here’s how eXp Realty for new agents is different:

  • Lead capture pages, automated follow-up, and text/email campaigns built-in.

Here’s where eXp Realty adds a layer most brokerages don’t even offer: sponsor teams. These aren’t just recruiters cashing checks  –  they’re agents who have real skin in the game when it comes to your success.

The brilliance of eXp’s sponsor model:

Sponsors at eXp Realty may choose to invest time, systems, or resources into the agents they sponsor. Because sponsor compensation is paid from company dollar rather than agent commissions, some sponsors provide structured onboarding, tools, or training, while others remain largely hands-off.

Simply put: when you win, they win. Unlike a traditional office where top producers may guard their secrets, great sponsor teams at eXp Realty are built to share exactly what’s working  –  because your growth fuels theirs.

What strong sponsor teams deliver:

  • Proven systems for lead generation, agent attraction, and non-transactional income growth
  • Additional real estate coaching, training, and strategy calls far beyond standard eXp corporate training
  • Done-for-you marketing templates, landing pages, and automation systems to generate leads while you’re focused on closings
  • Access to private masterminds, niche training (flipping, production, CRM mastery), and scalable business planning

Bottom line:
You’re not just joining a brokerage. With the right sponsor team, you’re plugging into an engine that’s designed to help you succeed  –  and has every reason to make sure you do.

How eXp Realty Reduces Early Career Friction For New Agents

Here’s the brutal truth: the 87% of real estate agents that fail, don’t fail because they’re bad at real estate. They fail because their bank account taps out before they ever get real traction. eXp Realty flips that script  –  not with hype, but with an actual blueprint.

You skip the “starving agent” phase.

  • Startup costs stay manageable, so you’re not burning through savings before your first deal.
  • The commission split stays 80/20 from day one  –  no punishing “rookie rates.”
  • You’re not playing the guessing game on where your next lead is coming from  –  your lead generation systems are already running.

You’re earning while you’re still learning.

  • From contract writing to client negotiations, mentorship and transaction support guide you through the early deals.
  • Instead of sitting through generic office meetings, you’re plugged into national masterminds and agent networks actually sharing what works right now.

You get full broker access, minus the overhead circus.

  • State brokers and tech support are available inside eXp World.
  • You collaborate with top agents across the country, not just the handful of people stuck at the same office desk as you.
  • And you avoid the desk fees, royalty fees, and politics that drain your income and your patience.

Systems build your business. Hype drains your wallet.
Franchise logos, office snacks, and feel-good slogans don’t close deals. New agents need:

  • Real estate training that leads to real production
  • Hands-on mentorship that stays involved
  • Marketing automation that attracts clients
  • Lead generation systems that scale
  • Affordable commission splits and low fees that leave money in your pocket
  • Broker support that actually answers

eXp Realty meets those new agent needs.

What Agents Also Ask About eXp Realty vs Traditional Brokerages for New Agents

Is eXp Realty harder for new agents than a traditional brokerage?

No. While eXp operates without physical offices, it provides structured mentorship, daily broker access, and extensive live training. New agents are supported through defined programs rather than informal office availability, which often varies widely at traditional brokerages.

Do new agents get enough guidance without an office environment?

Yes. eXp replaces office-based guidance with certified mentors, daily broker access, and structured live and on-demand training. New agents can receive transaction support, contract guidance, and business coaching consistently, without relying on inconsistent office hours or physical proximity to managers.

How does eXp help new agents get their first deals faster?

eXp provides structured mentorship, FastCap production systems, and built-in marketing tools. These systems focus on practical skills, lead generation, and transaction execution, supporting new agents as they move from licensing to completed transactions.

Who is eXp Realty best suited for among new agents?

eXp Realty works best for new agents who want systems, accountability, and scalable tools rather than office supervision. Agents willing to follow structured programs and engage with training tend to benefit more than those expecting non-transactional oversight.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty for new agents is designed to address use cases such as affordable startup costs, early deal support, lead generation access, and scalable training, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience. 

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is eXp Realty good for new real estate agents?

Yes. eXp Realty provides new agents with certified mentorship, daily broker access, and live and on-demand training delivered through centralized systems.Lower startup costs and built-in technology help reduce financial pressure while agents learn contracts, negotiations, and lead generation during their early transactions.

How much does it cost to join eXp Realty as a new agent?

New agents pay an $85 monthly technology fee, a one-time startup fee, an 80/20 commission split, and a $16,000 annual cap. After reaching the cap, agents earn 100% commission for the remainder of the year, reducing long-term cost exposure.

Do new agents get a mentor at eXp Realty?

Yes. Every new agent is assigned a local Certified eXp Mentor who provides transaction guidance, contract reviews, and business support for the first three deals. During mentorship, commission splits are adjusted, then revert to the standard 80/20 structure afterward.

Does eXp Realty offer live training for new agents?

Yes. eXp offers more than 60 live classes weekly, structured FastCap programs, and over 400 on-demand courses through eXp University. Training covers contracts, lead generation, marketing, negotiations, and business planning, supplementing mentor support.

Why does sponsor choice matter at eXp Realty for new agents?

Sponsor teams are not required to provide support, so value varies widely. Strong sponsors may offer coaching, systems, marketing tools, and accountability beyond corporate training. Evaluating sponsor resources can significantly impact how quickly new agents gain traction.

title: How to Find Buyers in Real Estate (Without Wasting Time on Duds)
excerpt: Key Points Most agents don’t need more leads – they need the right ones Buyer personas and niche marketing bring stronger results Local SEO and…
content:
Key Points

  • Most agents don’t need more leads – they need the right ones
  • Buyer personas and niche marketing bring stronger results
  • Local SEO and smart ads drive high-intent traffic
  • High-value content builds trust and captures leads
  • Video and social media increase engagement and conversions
  • Print, events, and open houses still work when done right
  • Fast, automated follow-up turns leads into appointments
  • Referral networks and CRM tools boost buyer pipeline
  • Incentives and buyer education improve conversion rates

If you’re tired of chasing ghost leads, talking to “just browsing” buyers, or refreshing your inbox like it owes you a closing – welcome. Most agents don’t struggle with finding people. They struggle with finding the right people. The ones who are ready to act, not just ask a thousand questions and disappear when it’s time to sign.

Want to learn how to find buyers in real estate who are actually serious? You don’t need gimmicks. You need a system. This guide walks you through exactly how top agents attract, connect with, and convert motivated buyers using a combo of smart targeting, modern marketing, and buyer psychology that doesn’t suck your soul dry.

Here’s your handy-dandy index:

Define and Target Your Ideal Buyers

Attract Buyers Online

Content That Converts Buyers

Offline Tactics That Still Work

Automated Follow-Up That Feels Personal

Real Relationships = Referral Machines

Advanced Buyer Conversion Techniques

Finding Real Buyers Isn’t Magic – It’s Method
Frequently Asked Questions

Define and Target Your Ideal Buyers

Start With Buyer Personas

Before you post another ad or build a landing page, stop. Who are you even trying to reach? Creating solid buyer personas means less random effort and more intentional results. For a step-by-step guide on developing real estate buyer personas, check out this resource from Milesbrand. Are you working with first-time buyers, move-up families, investors, or downsizers? You need to know their motivations, fears, timelines, and budgets like the back of your CRM.

Skip the surface stuff. Your personas should include:

  • Price range and preferred neighborhoods
  • Financing knowledge (or lack thereof)
  • Lifestyle goals (schools, walkability, fixer-uppers vs. turnkeys)
  • Dealbreakers and wishlist items

The better your buyer profiles, the easier it is to speak their language – and show up in their world before another agent does. Pro tip: If you don’t know how to get started on this task, open up ChatGPT and ask for it.

Tap Into Niche Segments

Generalists get general results. Want more serious, ready-to-act leads? Niche down. Explore the most profitable real estate niches and why they work in this article by Luxury Presence. Focus on groups like:

  • Eco-conscious buyers who prioritize green features
  • Luxury buyers with specific neighborhood tastes
  • Renters transitioning to first-time ownership
  • Multigenerational buyers looking for space with flexibility

These buyers have hyper-specific needs – and when your marketing speaks directly to them, they’ll skip the “research phase” and head straight to “let’s chat.”

Attract Buyers Online

Nail Your Local SEO

Most buyers start their journey on Google – not by calling an agent. If your online presence isn’t showing up when they search “condos in Your City” or “best Your City neighborhoods for families,” you’re invisible.

Use real estate SEO strategies to show up where it counts. BrightLocal offers tailored local SEO strategies specifically for real estate professionals. Show up where it counts:

  • Add location-based keywords to your blog, bio, and service pages
  • Write neighborhood guides and community breakdowns
  • Create landing pages for specific buyer types (free landing pages offer here).

And yes – claim and optimize your Google Business Profile. It’s free, it works, and it boosts your visibility in local map results.

Do Smart Keyword Research

Let’s kill the guesswork. Tools like Ubersuggest, Google Keyword Planner, and SEMRush show you what your buyers are searching for right now. Target keyword phrases that match your buyer personas, like:

  • “Homes with in-law units in Mill Valley”
  • “Low HOA condos for sale Marin”
  • “Best places to buy a house near SF commute”

This isn’t keyword stuffing – it’s showing up where serious buyers are looking.

Advertise With Intention

Random Facebook ads? Waste of money. Strategic, targeted ad campaigns? Goldmine.
Use:

  • Google Ads to grab high-intent search traffic
  • Facebook & Instagram ads to retarget site visitors and promote listings
  • A/B testing to tweak creative, headlines, and CTAs

Track your results like a hawk. No more throwing money into the void.

Optimize Your Landing Pages

Clicking your ad shouldn’t lead to a generic homepage. Build dedicated landing pages for:

  • First-time buyer guides
  • Investment property searches
  • Schedule-a-tour CTAs
  • “Best neighborhoods” lead magnets

Make them fast, mobile-friendly, and conversion-focused – or your clicks will bounce harder than a rent check in escrow. If you join our sponsor team, Smart Agent Alliance, we provide 8 landing page templates with automated email drips to keep you top of mind. This is how to get buyers while you’re doing something else.

Content That Converts Buyers

Create High-Value, Searchable Content

Want to get buyers in real estate who are already half-sold? Be the one who answers their questions before they even ask. Start building trust (and your email list) with:

  • Blog posts like “What Does a Buyers Agent Do?” or “Top 5 Questions to Ask a Buyers Agent”
  • Downloadables: real estate checklists for buyers, mortgage prep guides, or neighborhood comparisons
  • Market updates or property tour roundups

Not only does this content position you as the go-to expert – it helps warm up cold leads without spending a dime on ads.

Use Video to Educate and Engage

Let’s be real – no one’s reading a 3-paragraph property description when they could watch a walkthrough instead. Video is your cheat code for both buyers agent presentations and building likeability fast.

Use it to:

  • Break down the buying process (“What to Expect at Your First Buyers Consultation”)
  • Bust common myths (“Do Buyers Pay Realtor Fees?”)
  • Offer sneak peeks of listings or community features

Keep videos short, vertical, and full of personality. Bonus points if you go live for local Q&As or virtual open houses – buyers engage more when it feels real

Go All-In on Social Media

Posting isn’t enough. You need to connect. Build an ongoing relationship with future buyers by mixing:

  • Buyer success stories (yes, even if it’s “we got their offer accepted with zero drama”)
  • Tips, FAQs, and behind-the-scenes from your day
  • Local happenings, market shifts, or even memes that tie into buying

Instagram, Facebook, YouTube Shorts – whatever platform matches your persona, show up there. Social is your digital handshake. Keep it active, authentic, and invite engagement (polls, DMs, comments). See how to get social media training valued at over $10,500 below.

Keep the Value Coming

Buyer leads don’t stay warm on their own. Keep adding value even after they’ve downloaded your guide or DM’d about a listing. That means:

  • Weekly emails with curated listings or local intel
  • Invitations to webinars or workshops
  • Friendly check-ins if they ghosted last week’s showing invite

Be the agent who shows up with helpful stuff, not the one who only texts “still looking?” every three months.

Offline Tactics That Still Work

Use Print and Direct Mail With a Purpose

Yes, print still works – when you’re not mailing stuff that looks like it came from a 1998 Microsoft Publisher template. Even the National Association of REALTORS® still discusses the effectiveness of direct mail in real estate marketing. Create sleek, modern postcards or brochures that offer real value:

  • Market stats
  • Local inventory snapshots
  • Neighborhood spotlights
  • Invitations to buyer events

Include QR codes that link to your real estate buyers presentation or landing page. Then track responses and follow up like you actually want the client.

Host Open Houses Like a Lead Magnet

Stop treating open houses like passive weekend duty. These are IRL funnels – especially for buyers who haven’t picked their agent yet.

Treat them like mini-events:

  • Use sign-in apps to instantly drop visitors into your follow-up sequence
  • Invite local lenders to co-host (they love exposure)
  • Offer buyer resource sheets or exclusive previews

And, don’t miss top tips for successful open houses along with our free open house sheets.

Run Smart Local Events

Skip the basic happy hour. Run workshops that actually solve problems for your ideal buyers:

  • “First-Time Buyer Bootcamp”
  • “Buy vs. Rent in Today’s Market”
  • “How to Buy and Sell at the Same Time”

Partner with mortgage pros, title reps, or home inspectors. They get visibility, you get leads, and your attendees get answers. (Plus, it’s easier to schedule a buyers consultation when someone already met you at your event.)

Turn Sellers Into Buyers

Don’t forget – every seller needs to buy next. Offer a free pricing consultation that includes both a listing analysis and a buyer plan for their next move.

Follow up post-sale with helpful nudges about new listings in their price range, or connections to agents in their destination city if they’re moving out of area. Be the full-service buyers agent they didn’t know they needed.

Automated Follow-Up That Feels Personal

Use SMS and Email to Stay Top of Mind

Buyers today expect fast responses. And no, your once-a-month newsletter doesn’t count. To stay in the game, your follow-up needs to be fast, personal, and tailored.

Here’s how to keep buyers warm:

  • SMS: Great for instant reminders – open houses, price drops, or new listing alerts. Keep it short, sweet, and opt-in only.
  • Email: Segment by buyer type. First-time buyer? Send them a financing prep checklist. Luxury relocation client? Hit them with market data and off-market access.

The more relevant your content, the less likely it ends up in their trash folder.

Automate Like a Human (Not a Robot)

CRMs like Boldtrail, Follow Up Boss, or Chime can automate the grunt work without making you sound like a chatbot from 2009.

Set up:

  • Action-triggered emails (clicked a listing = “Want a tour?”)
  • Follow-up texts after form fills or downloads
  • Reminder sequences for your buyers consultation real estate appointments

Use merge tags to personalize messages and keep the tone casual – like you’re texting a friend, not selling them a timeshare.

Add Value on Autopilot

Your CRM shouldn’t just drip content. It should drip value. Build sequences that deliver:

  • Your real estate agent checklist for buyers
  • Video intros on what to expect from a buyer’s agent
  • Market watch reports or inventory snapshots

Most agents automate once and forget it. Great agents check performance monthly and adjust. You’re not a spammer. You’re their go-to guide through the chaos.

Real Relationships = Referral Machines

Build a Referral Network of Real Estate Pros

You know who talks to buyers before they even look at homes? Lenders. Inspectors. CPAs. Divorce attorneys. Make friends with these folks.

Get intentional:

  • Book 1-on-1 coffees
  • Trade referrals and follow up when they convert
  • Share each other’s content or co-host events

When they know your buyers consultation is smooth, informative, and not pushy, they’ll keep sending clients your way.

Treat Past Clients Like Future Clients

The deal closing isn’t the end – it’s the prequel. Stay in touch with past buyers by offering:

  • Annual home value updates
  • Market trend insights
  • Local events or homeowner checklists

Ask for reviews after you’ve done something helpful (like sending a reminder to file for their tax exemption). When you keep delivering, asking for a referral becomes a no-brainer.

Always Ask the Right Questions

Building real relationships means more than just saying “let me know if you need anything.” Use every buyer interaction – especially the buyers consultation – to uncover what matters.

Ask:

  • “What do you wish someone would just explain clearly?”
  • “What’s your biggest concern about buying?”
  • “Have you worked with an agent before?”

These aren’t just touchy-feely – they give you the intel to serve better and stand out. Because while most agents are still asking “What’s your budget?” you’ll be asking “What’s your long game?”

Advanced Buyer Conversion Techniques

Offer Buyer Education That Closes Deals

Let’s be honest – buying a home still feels like trying to do your own taxes in another language. Educated buyers close faster, trust more, and ghost less. Create resources that break down:

  • How financing works without making their eyes glaze over
  • Timeline expectations (your subtle way of avoiding “why is this taking so long?” texts)
  • What to expect from working with buyers in real estate, start to finish

Turn these into bite-size content – guides, checklists, short videos – and use them in your buyer nurture sequences, consultations, and social posts.

Pro tip: Hosting a “First-Time Buyer Workshop” or “How to Buy & Sell at the Same Time” event makes you the expert and the closer.

Sweeten the Deal With Incentives

Sometimes buyers need a gentle nudge (or a tiny bribe). Offering small but thoughtful perks can move people from “thinking about it” to “signing the offer.”

Try:

  • Covering a home warranty
  • Offering a limited-time closing cost credit
  • Throwing in a local service – like free junk removal or moving help

You don’t need to drop thousands. A well-timed realtor gift for buyers that solves an actual problem can seal the deal and make you unforgettable.

Turn One Buyer Into Five

This one’s simple math. Every happy buyer = a mini marketing team.
To activate them:

  • Ask for a review the moment they’re happiest (hint: not at closing – try right after their offer is accepted)
  • Send a thank-you gift they’ll post on Instagram (local wine, home décor, custom address stamp)
  • Invite them to join your referral program and make it feel exclusive

The result? Your buyers become brand ambassadors, and their network becomes your next pipeline.

Finding Real Buyers Isn’t Magic – It’s Method

Here’s the truth: learning how to find buyers in real estate isn’t about shouting into the void and hoping someone bites. It’s about using smart, targeted strategies that connect you with the right people – those who are motivated, serious, and actually want to buy a house.

From building buyer personas to automated follow-up that doesn’t feel like a robot wrote it, this isn’t about gimmicks – it’s about using modern tools, authentic content, and smart systems like other ideas in our agent success hub – to do the heavy lifting while you get back to, you know, actually working with buyers in real estate. 

And if you’re tired of playing the solo game, here’s where things get interesting…

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I find buyers in real estate without wasting time?

Start by defining clear buyer personas and targeting niche audiences. Use online and offline strategies like SEO, direct mail, and open houses that speak directly to serious buyers.

What does a buyers agent do for clients?

A buyers agent helps clients find homes, negotiates on their behalf, schedules tours, coordinates inspections, and guides them through every step of the purchase process.

What should I ask a buyers agent before hiring them?

Ask about their local experience, negotiation style, how they communicate, and their approach to helping buyers navigate financing, inspections, and contracts.

How can I follow up with buyer leads effectively?

Use a CRM to automate personalized emails, SMS, and reminders. Segment leads by buyer type and activity to deliver timely and relevant info that keeps them engaged.

What tools help real estate agents attract and convert buyers?

Use landing pages, buyer checklists, 3D home tours, video content, virtual open houses, and CRM software like kvCORE or Follow Up Boss to automate and scale your follow-up.

title: The Best Real Estate Brokerage for New Agents in 2025
excerpt: Key Points The best brokerage for new agents depends on training, support, and structure. Not all “big names” deliver – local office quality varies wildly.…
content:
Key Points

  • The best brokerage for new agents depends on training, support, and structure.
  • Not all “big names” deliver – local office quality varies wildly.
  • Cloud-based models like eXp Realty offer daily training and sponsor teams.
  • New agents can work solo, on teams, or with sponsor support (or all three).
  • Sponsor teams help without taking your split, unlike production teams.
  • Beware of fees – franchise, tech, and desk charges can eat your income.
  • Top success factors: training, mentorship, culture, tech, leads, and growth.
  • Look for structured mentorship, not “you’ll figure it out eventually.”
  • Your first year should be about support, not survival mode.

New agents don’t fail because they don’t want it bad enough. They fail because no one told them how much “figure it out” would be part of the job description. Between the fees, the “training” that’s just a PDF graveyard, and sponsors who vanish like your high school crush, it’s no wonder most agents tap out early.

So if you’re a new real estate agent trying to dodge the crash-and-burn phase, here’s your north star: pick the brokerage that actually helps you close deals – not just the one with the loudest TikTok dance.

Your success isn’t just about which brokerage you choose – it’s about how that brokerage sets you up to win. Training, tools, support, leads, and culture all matter. Here’s your handy-dandy index:

What is a Real Estate Broker (and Brokerage)?
Comparing Brokerage Models

Cloud-Based Brokerages
Traditional Franchise Brokerages
Local Independent Boutiques
Discount or Flat-Fee Models
Agent Roles: Solo, Team, or Sponsor Support

Solo Agent
Member of a Production Team
Solo Agent with a Sponsor
Member of a Sponsor Team
Commission Splits & Fees
Key Factors for New Agent Success

Choose the Brokerage That Sets You Up to Win
Frequently Asked Questions

What is a Real Estate Broker (and Brokerage)?

Let’s clear up the identity crisis. In real estate, the words broker and brokerage get tossed around like interchangeable salad toppings – but they’re not the same thing. And when you’re trying to figure out the best real estate brokerage for new agents, knowing the difference actually matters. A lot.

A real estate broker is a licensed pro who’s done the extra credit – more education, a tougher exam – and now has the legal authority to supervise agents or run their own show. They’re the ones who keep deals legal, contracts clean, and agents (mostly) out of trouble. Think of a broker as the designated driver of your real estate transactions.

A real estate brokerage, on the other hand, is a company under which brokers and agents work. The brokerage holds your license and provides your support structure, transaction systems, marketing tools, legal compliance, and—hopefully—some level of actual help.

So, why does this matter to you as a new agent?

Because picking the best real estate broker for new agents isn’t about the logo – it’s about the system behind it. A strong real estate brokerage gives you the training, tools, and support to survive those “what am I doing?” moments. One well-meaning broker can only help so much. The best brokerages offer real structure, real estate training for new agents, and resources that don’t vanish after your first week.

Comparing Brokerage Models

The best real estate brokerage for new agents isn’t one-size-fits-all. Different models come with different perks – and different landmines. Some offer full real estate coaching for new agents; others toss you a login and disappear. Here’s the short version of what you need to know:

Cloud-Based Brokerages

Think eXp Realty – no offices (but access to offices), no commute, just virtual training, strong tech, support from anywhere, and a whole lot of agent autonomy. It’s built for self-starters who want flexibility and efficiency. Wondering if eXp is good for new agents? Yep. eXp offers daily live training, onboarding, and sponsor teams to help with production, passive income, and community.

Traditional Franchise Brokerages

Big brands like RE/MAX and Keller Williams offer name recognition and in-person offices, but every location runs independently. One office might have stellar real estate training for new agents, another has zippo.

Expect lower commission splits (often 70/30 or worse), plus desk and franchise fees that chip away at your income. Ask detailed questions upfront – because you’re paying more, and what you get varies wildly. And, yes, online research about traditional brokerages is nearly impossible. Go to Glassdoor.com for some agent reviews.

Local Independent Boutiques

Usually run by a single broker, these offices might offer personalized mentorship, higher splits, and more autonomy than a franchise model. But training and tech depend entirely on the owner. If you like local vibes and direct access to leadership, this might be a fit. Just know that fewer staff means less support – and more is on you to figure out.

Discount or Flat-Fee Models

Low-cost, high-split models sound great – until you need help. These brokerages charge per transaction or monthly, but usually offer minimal training or tech. Best for experienced agents, not those learning how to convert leads or write clean contracts.

Agent Roles: Solo, Team, or Sponsor Support

Once you’ve picked the best real estate brokerage for new agents, you still need to figure out how you’ll actually work inside that brokerage. You’ve got a few role options – and each one comes with its own mix of support, splits, and day-to-day reality.

Let’s break down your three main paths: going solo, joining a team, or leveraging sponsor support.

Solo Agent

Most agents start out as solo agents. That means you’re running your own show under your brokerage’s umbrella. You pay the fees, generate the leads, and figure out your systems. You’re basically a one-person business with a real estate license and a dream.

This setup works best if your real estate brokerage for new agents actually offers strong onboarding, tech, and training. Otherwise, “solo” turns into “struggling in silence” – which is exactly why so many new agents tap out in year one. Some push through until the overwhelm hits, then pivot to a production team for support.

Member of a Production Team

Production teams are usually the first thing new agents hear about because they’re common inside most brokerages. These teams operate like mini-businesses designed to help agents buy and sell homes more efficiently. They typically provide leads, hands-on training, accountability, and a built-in sense of community. For many new agents, joining a team can be a fast-track to building confidence and closing deals—without having to reinvent the wheel on day one.

But here’s the catch: not all teams are created equal. Just like with franchise offices and independent boutique brokerages, some teams offer legit training and support – others hand you a script and wish you luck.

Also, teams come with their own split on top of your brokerage split. So while the leads and mentorship might flow, your paycheck will shrink accordingly. Before joining, ask questions about what you can expect and ask to see the team agreement – including how to exit. Some teams make it easy to leave. Others? Not so much.

Solo Agent with a Sponsor

This one’s unique to brokerages with a revenue share model, like eXp Realty. When you join, you name a sponsor – someone who benefits financially when you succeed. So in theory, they’re motivated to support you. In practice? Some do, some don’t. Everyone has time limits, so don’t count on endless hand-holding, especially from a solo sponsor.

The smart move? Choose a sponsor who offers actual value – like real estate coaching for new agents, marketing systems, or community – not just someone who slid into your DMs with “Let’s win together!” vibes.

Member of a Sponsor Team

Sponsor teams are also specific to brokerages with a revenue share model. These aren’t production teams – they don’t hold your hand through deals and most don’t take a split of your commissions. Instead, they provide tools, systems, and community designed to help you succeed as a solo agent. Think of it as an added layer of support – usually without an added layer of cost.

The catch? At brokerages like eXp Realty, you have to choose your sponsor team when you join eXp. After that, you’re locked in – unless you leave the company for a full year and come back. So yeah, doing your homework isn’t optional. On the upside, online research about eXp sponsors will help you decide.

Pick a sponsor team that’s organized, offers real tools and systems, and actually supports your production and revenue share goals. A strong community? That’s the frosting on your commission cake – and yeah, it matters!

Bonus: At eXp (and most other brokerages), sponsor team members can join a production team anytime. So if you start solo and realize you need a bit more structure (read: someone to boss you around in a helpful way), you can always become a production team member too. Just follow the advice above to make sure you’re picking the right team, not just the loudest one.

Commission Splits & Fees

We’ll keep this quick – our other blogs go deep on commission math, hidden fees, and how to not feel broke after your first closing. This section is just the essentials.

Commission splits are how your commission gets divided between you and your brokerage. The first number is your cut. So, if you’re on a 60/40 split, you keep 60%, and your brokerage takes 40%. Some brokerages use fixed splits, others use sliding scales that improve as you produce more. A few brokerages offer a cap – once you’ve paid in a set amount (like eXp’s $16K), you keep 100% for the rest of the year.

The tradeoff? Lower splits sometimes come with more support – like leads, training, and tech. Higher splits usually mean you’re more independent (read: figuring things out solo).

Now let’s talk fees – tech fees, desk fees, franchise fees, transaction fees. Some brokerages charge monthly. Others take a cut per deal. It all adds up fast, especially when you’re still ramping up. You can compare these for yourself using our brokerage comparison tool.

In any event, before you commit to a brokerage, ask for a detailed fee sheet: upfront, monthly, and per-transaction. Then check out our blogs on commission splits and fees so you know what you’re really taking home.

Key Factors for New Agent Success

Now that you’ve got a grip on brokerage types, splits, and fees, it’s time to talk about what really drives your success in this business. Because let’s be real – the best real estate brokerage for new agents isn’t the one with the flashiest office or the prettiest signs. It’s the one that actually sets you up to close deals, stay sane, and make it past year one.

A good fit means more than a name you recognize. It means real estate training for new agents that doesn’t suck, support when you hit a wall, and tools that actually help – not just look good in a sales pitch. These are the six key factors that separate thriving agents from the ones quietly ghosting their license renewal notices.

1. Training & Onboarding

Getting licensed teaches you how not to break the law. But getting trained teaches you how to actually sell houses.

Great brokerages start with a clear onboarding process (for example, here’s eXp’s) and continue with real estate coaching for new agents that includes contracts, negotiations, scripts, and even how to survive your first open house without crying in the bathroom. In competitive markets that includes local rules, city-specific disclosures, and hands-on practice – not just theory.

Look for brokerages that go beyond “read this PDF.” Weekly live sessions, role-playing, deal debriefs, shadowing top producers – that’s where the growth happens. And if they throw in a video library for after-hours studying? Bonus points.

2. Mentorship That’s Actually Useful

A true mentor doesn’t just sit near you and hope you learn by osmosis. The best real estate brokerages for new agents match you with someone who actually has time to answer questions, offer feedback, and walk you through awkward scenarios (like what to say when your buyer suddenly wants to “think about it” mid-escrow).

Ask if the mentorship is structured – how often you meet, what’s covered, and how mentors are selected. Vague “you’ll have support” promises are a red flag. You need someone who can spot your blind spots and help you build your skills, not just collect a fee on your closings.

3. Supportive Company Culture

New agents fail most often because they feel isolated and overwhelmed. That’s where company culture comes in.

A supportive brokerage means open-door managers, collaborative agents, tech help that actually shows up, and a place where asking questions isn’t treated like a weakness. Culture shows up in little things – who’s willing to share scripts, who answers your Slack messages, and whether there’s a community that makes you feel like you’re not doing this alone.

Before you join, talk to other agents. Ask what support really looks like. You’ll feel the vibe pretty quickly.

4. Tech That Works (And Helps)

Modern real estate runs on systems. Your brokerage should give you access to tools like a solid CRM, digital marketing tools, and listing tech that doesn’t look like it’s from 2005.

At minimum, look for:

  • CRM with follow-up automation (like Boldtrail or similar)
  • Email and text drips
  • Social media content tools
  • eSign and transaction management systems
  • Virtual office platforms for training and collaboration

Why? Because tech that works means less time in paperwork purgatory and more time closing deals – or, let’s be honest, generating leads to get there.

5. Lead Generation Support

Leads are the lifeline of a new agent’s business. The best real estate companies for new agents don’t just say “go find clients” – they help you do it (like Revenos at eXp).

That could mean PPC ads, relocation referrals, open house strategies, or sphere marketing tools. Some brokerages offer leads directly, others coach you on how to generate and convert them. The key is understanding what systems are in place – and what’s expected of you to make them work.

Ask: Are leads provided? Is there training on conversion? And do I pay extra for them?

6. Long-Term Growth Opportunities

Yes, it’s about closing your first deal – but it’s also about what happens after deal #10. The best real estate brokerage firms for beginners also help you plan your next chapter.

That might mean:

  • Coaching to scale into a team leader role
  • Management tracks or specialist positions
  • Continued education (marketing, negotiation, compliance)
  • Conferences, masterminds, and skill-building events

If your brokerage only focuses on the next deal, they’re not planning for your future. Ask about growth paths before you join.

Choose the Brokerage That Sets You Up to Win

There’s no one-size-fits-all answer to the best real estate brokerage for new agents – but there is one universal truth: support matters more than hype. You need a brokerage that actually trains you, supports you when you’re stuck, and helps you grow beyond just your first deal. We’ve got way more info to help you compare what different brokerages actually offer and most of it is nearly impossible to find through basic online searches.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the best real estate brokerage for new agents?

The best brokerage for new agents is one with strong training, mentorship, tech, and support.

Is eXp Realty good for new agents?

Yes, eXp Realty offers daily training, sponsor team support, and powerful virtual tools for new agents.

Should new agents join a team?

Joining a team can speed up learning and provide leads, but it also reduces your commission split.

What is a sponsor at eXp Realty?

A sponsor is the person you name when joining eXp – ideally a team of agents who offers tools, coaching, and support.

What fees should new agents watch out for?

Watch for marketing fees, franchise fees, desk fees, and per-transaction charges – they can quickly add up.

Do all brokerages offer training for new agents?

No. Some offer great training, others offer none. Always ask to see the training calendar before joining.

Can I switch my eXp sponsor if I made a mistake?

Not directly. You’d need to leave the company for a full year and rejoin to change sponsors. But eXp’s new co-sponsor opportunity can help you correct your mistake without leaving.

What’s the difference between a production team and a sponsor team?

Production teams often provide leads and take a commission split (on top of your brokerages). Sponsor teams offer systems and support, usually without taking your commission.
